title: VerifiedClaim
description: Schema for claims extracted from agent outputs. The Verification Layer validates each claim against the tier system before it reaches the frontend or trade plan.

claim_text.description: Human-readable claim as stated by the agent

claim_type.description: number=hard fact (revenue, EPS), ratio=calculated (P/E, EV/EBITDA), event=something happened (merger, earnings beat), opinion=subjective (bullish outlook), forecast=prediction (revenue growth 15%)

value.description: The claimed value. Number for numeric claims, string for events/opinions, null if not applicable.

period.description: Time period the claim refers to

source_primary.description: The original data source used by the agent

endpoint.description: Specific API endpoint or data path

source_verification.description: Second source used for cross-check. Null if not yet verified.

deviation_pct.description: Percentage deviation from primary. null if not numeric.

tier.description: Actual evidence tier achieved. A=primary/auditable (SEC filing, FRED), B=two aggregators consistent, C=single source/hint

required_tier.description: Minimum tier required based on usage. Trade-critical numbers need A or A+B. Context numbers need B. Sentiment needs C.

status.description: verified=Tier A confirmed (<5% deviation), consistent=2 aggregators agree, unverified=single source only, disputed=>5% deviation (RED FLAG), manual_check=Tier B available but claim is trade-critical (needs A)

trade_critical.description: If true, this claim directly influences buy/sell/hold decision. Disputed trade-critical claims BLOCK the trade plan.

confidence_adjustment.description: Points adjustment to overall analysis confidence. negative=reduces confidence. e.g. -15 for trade-critical claim with only Tier B.

examples.claim_text: AAPL is well-positioned for AI integration
